title: Reusable Workflows, Composite Actions, Interfaces, and Permission Flow · Release Evidence Brief: Ci Shared Automation Evolution
summary: Teams copy release YAML across repositories; a shared workflow changes an input silently, accepts all secrets, escalates permissions, and hides which workflow revision produced an artifact. Planned artifact: Add and verify the ci shared automation evolution increment in versioned reusable automation contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Evolve shared automation through semantic interface changes, canaries, deprecation, consumer inventory, rollback, telemetry, and migration ownership.
Pin external reusable workflows, record workflow ref and SHA, verify producer identity, and update through compatibility tests.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.
workspace: cicd
version-baseline:
  checkout: 7.0.0
  setup-node: 7.0.0
  upload-artifact: 7.0.1
  download-artifact: 8.0.1
  attest: 4.1.1
  node: 24-lts
  typescript-native: 7.0.2
  typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2
  build-push-action: 7
  google-auth: 3
  deploy-cloudrun: 3
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-delivery-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Predict Ci Shared Automation Evolution · Theory Ci Shared Automation Evolution
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory reusable workflow hides permissions and secrets, nests mutable dependencies, loses typed outputs, and silently changes callers. Design explicit inputs, outputs, authority, versioning, tests, and migration. Competency probe: Evolve shared automation through semantic interface changes, canaries, deprecation, consumer inventory, rollback, telemetry, and migration ownership.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Ci Shared Automation Evolution contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Ci Shared Automation Evolution · Theory Ci Shared Automation Evolution.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for evolve shared automation through semantic interface changes, canaries, deprecation, consumer inventory, rollback, telemetry, and migration ownership. For Predict Ci Shared Automation Evolution · Theory Ci Shared Automation Evolution, keep Ci Shared Automation Evolution connected to mechanically reviewable workflow, trust, artifact, deployment, and recovery evidence.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name event, workflow, revision, actor, permissions, runner, toolchain, dependency, artifact, environment, deployment, user-signal, and recovery boundaries; predict every state and trust transition; review the smallest deterministic workflow or release change; then verify a fault, repair, rollback, and authorized live-system transfer limit.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example reusable workflow hides permissions and secrets, nests mutable dependencies, loses typed outputs, and silently changes callers. Design explicit inputs, outputs, authority, versioning, tests, and migration. Competency probe: Evolve shared automation through semantic interface changes, canaries, deprecation, consumer inventory, rollback, telemetry, and migration ownership. A defensible solution applies Ci Shared Automation Evolution, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to versioned reusable automation contract.

content.code:
# Evidence: ci-worked-gha-reuse-composite-workflow-contracts-4
  - competency: ci-worked-gha-reuse-composite-workflow-contracts-4
    artifact: reusable-automation.yaml
    decision: worked gha reuse composite workflow contracts 4
    environment: GitHub-hosted ubuntu-24.04 runner on a cold cache
    fault-injection: expire the only retained test artifact during incident reconstruction
    retained-proof: accessible redacted timeline with owner expiry and regression control
    case-key: worked4
    change: |
      component: reusable-workflow
      workflow-call: typed-inputs-secrets-outputs
      permissions: caller-bounded
      secret-inheritance: explicit-only
      environment-secret: deployment-job-only
      external-ref: full-workflow-sha
      producer-proof: workflow-ref-and-sha
      compatibility: consumer-contract-suite
      deprecation: measured-migration
    verify: validate caller and callee interfaces permissions secrets outputs pins nested depth changed consumer cases and rollback
    failure: Reject wrapper equivalence, implicit secret inheritance, permission escalation, default-branch refs, or centralization as drift elimination.
    repair: Choose the correct boundary, type the interface, preserve caller limits, pin identity, test consumers, canary change, and deprecate visibly.
content.caption: Runnable CI/CD workflow, artifact, release, and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Centralizing a workflow automatically eliminates configuration drift. This fails the Ci Shared Automation Evolution contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

steps.title: Build Ci Shared Automation Evolution evidence · Theory Ci Shared Automation Evolution

Tailor its reusable-automation.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic workflow or release review, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run GitHub Actions, runner, Docker, registry, OIDC, Google Cloud, and deployment work later in authorized disposable transfer environments.
